author: jtraglia
content: Interesting. Why 12? Wouldn't 16 be more appropriate? There are two index values, so 2x makes sense to me.

author: nflaig
content: yeah 16 is probably right

author: jtraglia
content: Raising MAX_ATTESTATIONS

author: nc1234
content: This only triggers when there are skipped slots right? Normal scenario data.index should mostly be zero. So not just straight 2x

author: nflaig
content: was thinking along those lines

author: tersec
content: oh, I know an EIP which does this: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7688#converted-types

author: nflaig
content: it's almost like Etan trying to sell it as a solution for the deposit limit

author: nflaig
content: but this one I don't get, you'd still limit blocks to 8 attestations?

author: tersec
content: yes, almost, what a coincidence

author: tersec
content:
> BeaconBlockBody
> 
>     The proposer_slashings, attester_slashings, attestations, deposits, voluntary_exits and bls_to_execution_changes fields are redefined to use ProgressiveList

author: nflaig
content: so we can have infinite attestations now? 😄

author: tersec
content: the theory I think is that what should be limited is amount of BLS verifying or other cost function

author: tersec
content: plus overall message size

author: nflaig
content: but that's why we set it to 8 in electra

author: tersec
content: I'll say that actually this aspect is one aspect of 7688 which needs particular care. so, historically, a lot of fields were semi-accidentally limited to reasonable values. sometimes those reasonable values are yeah, reasonable. sometimes they're big and waste a lot of merkle tree depth/hashing. sometimes they turn out to be too small (the deposit requests)

author: tersec
content: but either way there were built-in DoS resistances

author: tersec
content: that needs explicit thought/planning now, exactly like the ELs do

author: tersec
content: like, e.g., <@755590043632140352> already explicitly talking about (separately from 7688 or any specific representation, just conceptually) that he disagrees with the notion of limiting those at all and prefers cryptoeconomic measures, i.e. if someone is willing to burn 1 ETH to orphan one envelope, so be it

author: tersec
content: or rather 1 ETH * 8192 or whatever

author: tersec
content: for deposit requests

author: nflaig
content: that's fine for deposits

author: nflaig
content: but we would still enforce `MAX_ATTESTATIONS_ELECTRA` as is, we can have a `MAX_ATTESTATIONS_GLOAS` but that's completely unrelated to EIP-7688

author: tersec
content: yeah

author: tersec
content: it would at least make it less painful to change

author: tersec
content: because it would be just a detached/decoupled runtime limit

author: tersec
content: not an SSZ type

author: nflaig
content: that is true becasue you don't touch the container

author: tersec
content: which is a general point about 7688: just because the limits aren't built into the types anymore doesn't mean they can't or shouldn't exist

author: tersec
content: and in fact they can be more flexible

author: tersec
content: the first level of DoS resistance is still `MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE`

author: tersec
content: so it can't really get more pathological than it already can today

author: tersec
content: we can also say, that the number of BLS signatures can't exceed `n`, for some `n`. that's not a constraint expressible now

author: nc1234
content: Right, so we discuss the value of the attestation limit here first, then see where to place that limit (whether 7688 is adopted)

author: tersec
content: fair enough

author: nflaig
content: cc <@425572898787426305> I know we had this topic before during the holesky incident and decided 8 is fine, but worth to revisit/reconsider for gloas

author: nflaig
content: seems like there has been a lot of discussion about this before

author: tersec
content: yeah, which means if there are advantages they'd have to be quantitatively measurable also

author: tersec
content: Also, <@425572898787426305> showed that chart in a pre-ePBS world, where envelopes were part of the block. Now that they aren't, even if the absolute change would be similar, the relative change is larger

author: tersec
content: one might start asking, is it worth doubling or tripling the block size for this, etc. Because that ... doesn't look very minor to me

author: tersec
content: when people are talking abour 4s slots and 6s slots

author: tersec
content: and 2s or less end-to-end to do it in

author: etan_status
content: yep, and because it doesn't touch the container, we could also just limit the overall signature count and, e.g., allow more slashings to be included when there are no bls to execution changes eating up sig slots 🙂  it moves all the logic from the static data type to whatever rule you want

author: etan_status
content: another unlock is finally getting the abiltiy to delete legacy fields without annoying staking pools etc: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8015

author: etan_status
content: but yeah, 7688 solely moves the limit from the datatype to logic, it doesn't change anything except hashes on its own, it doesn't change limits etc
